blocks.content: Where is Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Olivia suddenly stopped as if she had just remembered something.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: This won't do. I will personally observe the afternoon classes. I'll go through all the classes starting from Class Ten, so please prepare.

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: Yes, understood.
Thad answered as if he was under a spell. It was hard to resent someone who was genuinely trying to raise the academy's reputation.
As Olivia hurried her steps again, Shirone approached her from across the hallway. It was still early for him to have finished lunch, so Thad tilted his head and asked,

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: Shirone? What's the matter?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, hello.
Olivia looked closely at Shirone as he approached. This was the boy Alpheas had mentioned as resembling himself. But the Alpheas she knew wasn't like this.

Well, she had met him when he was in his early thirties, around the time he lost his wife and became a wreck. So, perhaps this boy represented the light of Mirhi Family, the way Alpheas had been before marriage.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: You must be Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, it's a pleasure to meet you.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Indeed. It must be lunchtime now, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I wanted to meet, Principal. I went to the principal's office, but he wasn't there.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Of course. Starting today, I am the principal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, I'm sorry.
When Shirone realized his slip of the tongue and panicked, Thad quickly intervened and hushed up the situation.
Thad motioned for him to hurry up. Realizing what was happening, Shirone nodded and went back the way he came.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Phew! That was suffocating.
Once outside, Shirone took a deep breath.
The aura emitted by the second-rank archmage was something he had never experienced before.
He had felt pressure from Arcane, but Olivia surpassed even that. Only after leaving her presence did he realize he hadn't been breathing properly.
After calming down, Shirone went to the annex as Thad had told him. He grabbed the doorknob and knocked on the door, and Alpheas, dressed in casual attire, greeted him.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I've been expecting you. Come in.
Shirone looked around the simple annex and then fixed his gaze on the portrait hanging on the wall.
Alpheas pointed to it and introduced it.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: My wife, Erina. You've heard about her, haven't you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. Hello.
Shirone bowed to the portrait. For some reason, he felt like he had to do it.
Alpheas raised an eyebrow at the odd gesture but didn't tell him to stop.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Right, I'm sure you have a lot to talk about. But before we get into that… Are you hungry?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A bit, actually.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I made some mushroom soup. Let's eat together.
The two sat across from each other and ate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Principal, I've been to Heaven.
Clang
Alpheas, who had soup trickling down his chin, hurriedly swallowed and wiped his beard.
He couldn't believe it, but when he looked at Shirone's face, it was true. It wasn't the face of a traveler who had been to an amazing place or an explorer who had discovered something incredible.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: When you say Heaven...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry. It just happened that way. Canis...
Alpheas was silent for a while after hearing the whole story.
Especially since this was the first he had heard that Arcane had gone to Heaven.
Indeed, looking back, he was a man of many secrets. He chose evil over good and evil, but he was a person close to nihilism.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Principal, I have a question. How did Miro come to be in charge of that task?
Alpheas realized Shirone's guess. But his thoughts did not change. It was already in the past and couldn't be undone. One person dwelling in the past was enough.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Gaold...

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Micaea Gaold. Now considered a First-Class Archmage in the kingdom, but to Alpheas, he remained a pure-hearted young man, forever his disciple in memory.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Shirone, don't try to learn everything. This is a supranational matter. The moment you step into it, your life will get entangled.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But if Miro...!

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: What difference does it make if you know?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone kept his mouth shut. Knowing wouldn't change anything. Even now, this world was maintained by the strength of just one person.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Principal, if army of Heaven breaks through Miro's space-time...
Alpheas shook his head.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: After Miro blocked Heaven's army, think tanks from various countries have been preparing for a long time. Do you understand, Shirone? Nothing will change. You have your dreams. Don't ruin your life by being tied down to untouchable truths.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, don't worry. I felt it deeply too.
Alpheas smiled kindly.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Ataraxia is impressive, even to me. In ancient times, the abilities of angels were called dragon-slaying magic. If you have the chance later, look up the history of the dragon race. They have a much longer history than humans and can tell you more about angels.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone unexpectedly obtained some good information. Up until now, he had only thought about the relationship between Heaven and humans, but when he looked closely, it was an issue intertwined with the entire world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I need to study the history of other races too.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Should I take responsibility for this power?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Hoho, Shirone, there's no such thing. No one knows what results your choices will bring. You must think and decide for yourself.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I hesitate so many times.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Hohoho! That's a natural phenomenon. You can say that you're stronger than me at least for the moment Ataraxia is unfolded.
Alpheas' words were not a lie. If Shirone's story was not an exaggeration, the moment the Ataraxia magic circle opened, it would have been a power that even a certified 4th-rank mage could not stop.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But Shirone wasn't happy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Because he knew what Alpheas meant by the prerequisite, 'The moment Ataraxia is unfolded.'

Theory and reality are two completely different things. Leaving the Immortal Function open and defenseless for a minute is no different than sticking your neck out and waiting to be cut down in real life.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I understand what Principal is trying to say. It's true that it's a power that's still overwhelming for me. I will train diligently and make it my own.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Yes. You have found a good connection. It is yours. Embrace it and ponder it deeply.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, thank you.
When he confessed to Alpheas, his heart felt much lighter. It might seem like passing the responsibility to an adult, but it was natural.
What Shirone could do was to grow up quickly and share their responsibilities.
As Shirone returned to academy, Alpheas locked the door and returned with a serious expression.
It was new information about Heaven after a year.
When Miro returned from Heaven, she accepted the reality that she had to save everyone at the cost of her sacrifice.
But Shirone was different. He fought against heaven.
Conflict creates enemies, but it also creates allies.
Shirone gained a comrade in return for his struggle in Heaven. The world will take notice of his achievements that have a different meaning from Miro's.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: My dear, honestly, I am afraid. Now I don't know who the child is.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Ataraxia. He said irresponsible things to him, telling him to embrace it and handle it himself.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: But giving advice on a power one can't handle isn't his nature.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: It's been a long time since my blood boiled like this. Saying this might make you sad, but I wonder if my mistake of letting you go was to meet this boy someday.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: He didn't think that he was the only one who could teach Shirone, but his failures in his younger days would certainly serve as a foundation for Shirone's growth.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: What do you think? Can I be a little more greedy?
The portrait of Erina was smiling.

Arin was enjoying a pleasant stroll through the flower garden behind the advanced class section, where the autumn flowers were in full bloom. It was a narrow, secluded space that no one visited, but she liked it even more because of that.
Even though most of the lessons were things she already knew, adjusting to an academy for the first time in her life was more challenging than expected.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin, were you waiting long?
Canis came running with a rice ball in his arms. Although Alpheas had paid for their tuition, including board and lodging, he didn't want to rely on the academy's provisions.
It may have been childish stubbornness to refuse meals while using the lodging, but Alpheas did not stop him.
It's the academy that killed Arcane. Even without mentioning his young age, his determination to keep his sense of duty to their master was admirable.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Let's eat. I hope I got the seasoning right this time.
Arin sat in the flower bed and ate the rice ball. Initially, they were too salty to eat, but the taste was gradually improving.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Maybe I should get a night job or something.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis, Principal is giving you an allowance. It seems stupid to be stubborn.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I know. But the moment you think it's a given, it's over. We might have to accept some things, but we should gradually refuse. I don't want to rely on it.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: If it gets really desperate, we'll use it. But I don't want to rely on it.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Tch, not here either. Where the hell did they go?
Dante and his crew entered the garden.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Should we ask?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hey, are you guys from Class Four?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Top? The top is Shirone, isn't it?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Is this guy also Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Following Iruki, Canis also pointed out Shirone. Finally, it felt like the pieces were coming together. Master Olivia had pinpointed it accurately.
